title: eriogonum luteolum var. luteolum
intro: Eriogonum luteolum var. luteolum, also known as the desert trumpet or yellow buckwheat, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Eriogonum luteolum var. luteolum include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water, or by using the plant's roots and stems to create a tincture.
